metadata.title: Contested resource states — Venezuela, Greenland, Argentina, Iran across materials, military, capital & surveillance
metadata.status: events FACT (filings/government/major-press); the 'resource-securing turns kinetic/coercive' framing is labeled interpretation; intent not inferred from adjacency
metadata.frame: Four peripheries where the great-power contest over materials and energy turned, in 2025-26, into military force, financial capture, or sanctions war. The throughline this project cares about: the SAME chokepoint logic proven for compute (defense_chokepoint: independence infeasible until ~2028) is now driving the US to secure physical resource access by force (Venezuela oil/gold/REE), territory (Greenland REE/Arctic), money (Argentina lithium via a $40B bailout), and denial (Iran - strikes + sanctions to cut China off the oil/REE axis). Overlay; excluded from the proofs. Connects to defense_web, allied_mincut, macro-critical-minerals-equities, macro-stablecoin-treasury-rail, macro-gold-silver-reprice, digitalid-regulatory.

venezuela.event: US captured President Maduro on 3 Jan 2026 ('Operation Absolute Resolve'), after a Caribbean military buildup since Sep 2025 ('Operation Southern Spear'), a Dec-2025 naval blockade, and tanker seizures (USS Gerald R. Ford). Maduro flown to NYC for a drug-trafficking trial. status: fact.
venezuela.materials: Venezuela holds the world's largest proven OIL reserves + large GOLD (much mined illegally and used for sanctions evasion) and rare earths. Hours after the capture, Trump stated the operation was 'at least in part' about oil: 'our very large US oil companies... go in, spend billions... start making money.' Chevron is currently the only US operator. status: fact (the statements) + contested (oil as the primary motive vs drug/regime framing).
venezuela.guyana_essequibo: Guyana oil output OVERTOOK Venezuela on 19 Dec 2025; US majors are arguably more interested in protecting ExxonMobil's offshore Guyana/Suriname oil from Venezuela's Essequibo claim than in Venezuelan crude itself. status: fact.
venezuela.read: the first 21st-century US regime-change operation framed on narcotics but stated by the President to be about oil - resource access secured by force. Cross-ref macro-gold-silver-reprice (Venezuelan gold) and the energy leg.

greenland.event: Trump announced a 'framework of a future deal' on Greenland with NATO's Rutte at Davos (Jan 2026), reviving the 2019 purchase push (annexation rhetoric). status: fact (framework announced) + contested (what it contains).
greenland.materials: Estimated 36-42 million tonnes of rare-earth oxides - potentially the world's SECOND-largest reserve after China (Tanbreez/CRML, Kvanefjeld). But harsh-climate economics make near-term mining marginal (cf. macro-critical-minerals-equities: CRML pre-revenue ~2028-29). status: fact (reserves) + contested (economic viability).
greenland.military_intel: The US already runs PITUFFIK SPACE BASE (NW Greenland) - missile-warning radar for homeland defense - and Greenland anchors the GIUK GAP, the Arctic surveillance chokepoint (Greenland-Iceland-UK). China declared itself a 'Near-Arctic State' (2018) and bid on Greenland airport construction. status: fact.
greenland.cost: Acquisition/support estimated in the 'hundreds of billions' (replacing Denmark's ~$600M/yr subsidy + infrastructure + safety net). status: contested (estimates).
greenland.read: territory pursued for REE + Arctic military/early-warning position - the materials chokepoint and the surveillance chokepoint in one island. Ties to geopolitics-allied-intel-nodes (Arctic).

argentina.event: Under Milei, the Trump administration extended a ~$40B backstop ($20B currency SWAP LINE + ~$20B from private banks/sovereign-wealth funds), atop a $20B IMF program; the RIGI large-investment regime anchors mining/energy projects. status: fact.
argentina.materials: Argentina holds ~20% of global identified LITHIUM resources (the Lithium Triangle) + Vaca Muerta shale (record 849,646 bpd crude Oct 2025, ~70% of national output). Notably, despite the US pivot, Milei approved RIGI incentives for a $1.24B expansion of a Jujuy lithium mine half-owned by China's GANFENG - so China retains a lithium foothold even as the US underwrites the macro. status: fact.
argentina.capital: Inflation fell 211% (2023) -> 31.5% (end-2025); full dollarization stalled (~3% market-implied probability by mid-2026); the bond market still doubts the recovery. The US backstop is a strategic bet to pull Argentina (and its lithium) out of China's orbit. status: fact + contested (durability).
argentina.read: the contested-alignment case: resource access secured by MONEY (a US bailout + IMF), not force - but China keeps a lithium seat at the table. Cross-ref macro-stablecoin-treasury-rail / the IMF-as-instrument theme.

iran.event: June 2025: US/Israel struck Iran's nuclear sites (B-2 MOPs on Fordow; Tomahawks on Natanz/Esfahan) - the '12-Day War'; damage assessed as setting the program back 'months' (IAEA's Grossi: rebuildable). Escalated into a 2026 Iran war (since 28 Feb 2026): strikes assassinated Supreme Leader Khamenei; Iran retaliated on Israel, US bases and Gulf states and moved to block the STRAIT OF HORMUZ -> a global fuel crisis; active strikes ongoing into June 2026. status: fact.
iran.materials_china_axis: China buys ~90% of Iran's oil, mostly via independent 'teapot' refineries; US Treasury/OFAC sanctioned China's Hengli Petrochemical (and the shadow fleet) in 2026. Iran's 'resistance economy' is building domestic REE/lithium (a monazite-processing plant launched Apr 2025) to link into a Eurasian (China+Russia) supply chain bypassing Western sanctions. status: fact.
iran.surveillance: Iran's intelligence services sharply expanded DOMESTIC surveillance after the strikes - IRGC warned citizens that Instagram/Telegram are monitored, cross-referencing social activity against dissident profiles - layered on the National Information Network / 'white-SIM' tiered internet (see digitalid-regulatory). status: fact.
iran.read: the denial case: force + sanctions used not only on Iran's nuclear program but to cut CHINA off the Iranian oil + REE axis - the chokepoint contest fought on a third country's territory; Hormuz shows the energy chokepoint's global blast radius.

the_pattern: Four instruments for one objective - securing (or denying an adversary) physical resources the post-industrial/AI/defense economy needs: FORCE (Venezuela oil), TERRITORY (Greenland REE + Arctic), MONEY (Argentina lithium via bailout), DENIAL (Iran strikes + sanctions vs the China axis). It is the geopolitical extension of the same finding the formal core proves about compute: the binding constraints are PHYSICAL (energy, rare earths, location), not financial - so when money can't buy past them, states reach for force, territory, and sanctions instead. Resource nationalism with great-power characteristics.
